---
title: "Nvidia drivers after upgrade"
date: 2007-11-15
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by detectiveinspekta on 2007-11-15
Was using edgy and decided to upgrade. Previously I had beryl working fine with the nvidia drivers.
I get the blue screen X messages when I try to boot with the lastest kernel. The splash screen has changed it I think its just something wrong with the display drivers again.

My /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Fri Dec 15 10:12:14 PST 2006

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"

no screens found


I have tried to install the latest Nvidia drivers (100 vers) with no success. What is the problem?

---

### Post by detectiveinspekta on 2007-11-15
Ok things are working now. I just needed to type startx.

But I fear that this is the same problem as I had ages ago where I had to install the driver everytime I had to startx.

---
